title: StoryGuard Requirements Completeness Scoring Rubric
description: 5-dimension rubric, 2 points per dimension, 10 points maximum. A story scoring below 8.0 is not sprint-ready. Use this rubric in the PROMPT_06 governance and scoring step.

sprint_ready.action: Proceed to sprint after BA approval

needs_review.action: BA must address improvement suggestions before sprint entry

incomplete.action: Return to stakeholder for clarification on missing fields

blocked.action: Do not proceed — intake insufficient for story creation

dimensions.name: Story Clarity
dimensions.description: Is the user story specific, unambiguous, and actionable?

0.label: Vague
criteria: Story contains language like 'improve', 'better', 'handle', 'appropriate', 'ensure quality'
The 'I want' statement could be interpreted multiple ways by different engineers
Persona is 'user' or 'stakeholder' rather than a named role
Business value in 'so that' is not measurable or observable

1.label: Mostly Clear
criteria: Story is mostly specific with 1-2 minor ambiguities that a senior engineer could reasonably interpret
Persona is named but may be too broad (e.g., 'employee' vs 'financial advisor')
Business value is stated but not measurable

2.label: Implementation Ready
criteria: No vague language — every term is specific and unambiguous
Persona is a specific named role with clear context
The 'I want' has exactly one reasonable interpretation
Business value is measurable or directly observable

dimensions.name: Non-Functional Requirements Coverage
dimensions.description: Are NFRs defined across all 5 categories appropriate to the domain?

0.label: Missing
criteria: Fewer than 2 NFR categories addressed
All NFRs marked as MISSING with no specifics
Performance, security, compliance all absent for a story that clearly requires them

1.label: Partial
criteria: 2-3 NFR categories addressed with specific requirements
At least 1 NFR confirmed or inferred with a specific threshold
Some NFRs marked MISSING but with clear questions for the stakeholder

2.label: Complete
criteria: All 5 NFR categories addressed (performance, security, compliance, reliability, auditability)
Each NFR has a specific, measurable requirement or a clear 'MISSING: [question]'
Compliance requirements include specific regulation names where applicable
Auditability requirements defined for all stories touching financial data

dimensions.financial_services_note: In wealth management and financial services, auditability is rarely 'not_applicable'. When in doubt, require it.

dimensions.name: System Dependency Identification
dimensions.description: Are upstream and downstream system dependencies mapped with risk context?

0.label: Not Mapped
criteria: No upstream or downstream dependencies identified
No data sources identified for a story that clearly involves data retrieval or storage

1.label: Partially Mapped
criteria: Primary upstream dependency identified but secondary dependencies missing
Downstream impacts identified but without risk context
Data sources identified but PII flag not assessed

2.label: Fully Mapped
criteria: All upstream dependencies identified with dependency type and risk note
All downstream systems identified with impact description
External integrations named where applicable
Data sources identified with read/write operation and PII flag
All dependencies have a validation status (confirmed, inferred, requires_validation)

dimensions.name: Acceptance Criteria Testability
dimensions.description: Are acceptance criteria binary, specific, and independently verifiable?

0.label: Untestable
criteria: Criteria contain subjective language ('the system works correctly', 'performance is acceptable')
Fewer than 3 acceptance criteria
No error handling or edge case criteria
Then statements are not observable

1.label: Mostly Testable
criteria: 3-4 acceptance criteria, mostly binary
Happy path covered but edge cases or error handling missing
Some criteria are specific but 1-2 have subjective language

2.label: Fully Testable
criteria: Minimum 4 criteria: 2 happy path, 1 edge case, 1 error handling
Every criterion is binary — unambiguous pass or fail
At least 1 compliance or audit criterion for financial services stories
All 'Then' statements are specific and observable
Negative tests documented for critical error scenarios

dimensions.name: Assumption Transparency
dimensions.description: Are AI inferences documented with validation questions for the BA?

0.label: Opaque
criteria: No assumptions documented
AI-generated content presented as fact without distinguishing inferred from stated
No missing information flagged

1.label: Partially Transparent
criteria: Some assumptions documented but not all
Missing information flagged but without specific questions for the stakeholder
Hallucination flags missing for high-risk inferences

2.label: Fully Transparent
criteria: Every AI inference documented as an assumption with confidence level
Each assumption has a specific validation question for the BA
Hallucination flags present for any claim not supported by intake
Missing information listed with exact questions to ask the stakeholder
The BA can clearly distinguish what was stated vs inferred vs assumed

score_interpretation.10.0: Exceptional — story could go directly to engineering with minimal BA review
score_interpretation.8.0-9.9: Sprint ready — BA review and approval recommended before sprint entry
score_interpretation.6.0-7.9: Needs improvement — address flagged items before sprint entry
score_interpretation.4.0-5.9: Incomplete — requires stakeholder follow-up on multiple gaps
score_interpretation.2.0-3.9: Significantly incomplete — return to stakeholder before continuing
score_interpretation.0.0-1.9: Blocked — intake insufficient, start elicitation process over

baseline_comparison.description: Use this to measure StoryGuard's impact. Score the raw intake before enrichment, then score the enriched output.
baseline_comparison.how_to_score_raw_intake: Apply the same rubric to the raw intake text as if it were a requirements document. Most raw intakes score 0-1 on NFRs, 0 on dependencies, 0-1 on acceptance criteria, 0 on assumptions.
